title: lotus scoparius subsp. traskiae
intro: Lotus scoparius subsp. traskiae, also known as Trask's lotus or Trask's yellow lotus, is a subspecies of the Lotus scoparius plant, a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Baja California. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lotus scoparius subsp. traskiae include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting overall well-being, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
